---
title: "Ren SATO"
lang: en
---


## Affiliation

Doctoral Program, Department of Bioengineering, Graduate School of Engineering, The University of Tokyo (from FY2026)

Katashima-Chung Lab.


## Research

**○ The relationship between tactile cognition and rheological property**

![Schematic illistration of my reserch theme](OverView.png)

・Soft matter, exemplified by the human body, foods, and cosmetics, exhibits intermediate properties between viscosity and elasticity, known as **“rheological properties”**.<br>
→ diverse forms of tactile perception, such as “puffy” or “sticky”.

・The tactile feel of soft matter varies depending on the material properties, the contact conditions, and the manner of touching.<br>
→ This indicates the need to investigate how rheological properties and the context/background of participants affect touching behavior, tactile perception, and affective evaluation, which would contribute to product development and to elucidating haptic cognition.

・Soft matter possesses hierarchical structures ranging from the Å scale to the μm scale, and these structures affect rheological properties <br>
→ The range of materials that can be compared systematically is limited.<br>
→ This motivates the use of [Tetra-PEG slime](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.progpolymsci.2025.102042) as a model material whose structure can be controlled.

Keyword: Rheology · Psycho-rheology · Cognition science · Polymer physics · Gel · Transient network

## Contact

E-mail (Personal): rensato.ut[at]gmail.com

E-mail (University): sato-ren3100[at]g.ecc.u-toyko.ac.jp

## Links
- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.co.jp/citations?user=0wo5w1IAAAAJ&hl=ja&oi=ao)
- [Lab. information](https://rheo.tokyo/)
